content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended Memory v3 write and read architecture flow; The recommended architecture logs User / Tool / Document input in Source Log, routes it through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval, canonicalizes entities, dispatches to Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, reconciles claims and notes into Postgres + pgvector, runs background consolidation/repair/retention workers, and serves runtime answers through Read-Time Retrieval Planner and Answer Composer.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Memory write model separates semantic and maintenance paths; The Memory v3 write model uses two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths: Direct structured path for safely normalizable facts, preferences, tasks, and status changes; Note path for important but not-yet-hard facts such as rationale, trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons, document digests, and contextual framing; Consolidation promotes mature notes when signal becomes strong enough; Repair fixes memory degradation.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- claim hot-path:claim:<0> primary_storage_backend; The recommended Memory v3 architecture uses Postgres + pgvector as its primary storage backend.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 4 recommended architecture
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer needs to store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_semantic_write_path; The Memory v3 system has Note path as a semantic write path.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3-4
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_constraint; The AI agent memory layer must not turn every turn into permanent knowledge.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> converts_mature_notes_into_memory_representations; Consolidation converts mature notes into claims, tasks, profile, or episodes when the signal is strong enough.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> includes_representation_type; The Representation axis of the memory architecture includes Note as a representation type.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_architecture_axis; Memory v3 architecture has Scope and lineage as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_semantic_write_path; The Memory v3 system has Direct structured path as a semantic write path.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3-4
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_architecture_axis; Memory v3 architecture has Retrieval policy as one of its four axes.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> includes_representation_type; The Representation axis of the memory architecture includes Claim as a representation type.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> includes_representation_type; The Representation axis of the memory architecture includes Episode as a representation type.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> includes_representation_type; The Representation axis of the memory architecture includes Source as a representation type.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> includes_representation_type; The Representation axis of the memory architecture includes Task as a representation type.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<13> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer needs to survive across the boundary of a single session.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<14> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer needs to be able to age, archive, and repair memory.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<15> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer needs to be able to explain where a memory came from.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<16> includes_representation_type; The Representation axis of the memory architecture includes Profile as a representation type.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- claim hot-path:claim:<17> has_goal; The AI agent memory layer needs to support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'

Raw evidence Immutable или почти immutable слой: - chat turns - tool outputs - imported records ### Tier E. Retrieval indexes Даже если физически все сидит в одном Postgres: - vector indexes на `sources`, `notes`, `claims`, `episodes` - full-text indexes на `content_text`, `summary`, `normalized_text` - filters по `namespace`, `entity`, `status`, `retrieval_state`, `time`, `type` ## 6. Нужно хранить одновременно: - `scope_text` - человеческое объяснение границ - `scope_json` - машинно-обрабатываемый envelope Минимум в `scope_json`: - `scope_kind` - `project_id` - `environment` - `subject_entity_id` - `conversation_id` - `modality` - `audience` Почему это важно: - reconciliation становится предсказуемее - retrieval меньше переобобщает - notes не конфликтуют ложно только потому, что обе “про базу”

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:f5dcd6c9392e5433046cb73719dbe2d9] EXTERNAL
Source metadata: source_kind=document; origin=pasted_document_section; document_type=MARKDOWN; source_ref=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; title=01. Architecture; heading=01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope; imported_at=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope
Document lines: 127-208

## 5. Storage tiers

### Tier A. Core / in-context memory
Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt.

Что туда класть:
- краткий user profile
- active tasks
- очень короткий session summary
- system-level behavior preferences

Что туда не класть:
- все claims подряд
- все notes подряд
- raw transcript
- длинные списки фактов

### Tier B. Structured long-term memory
Основное typed memory-хранилище.

Типы:
- `Entity`
- `Claim`
- `Profile`
- `Task`
- `Episode`
- `Source`
- `MemoryRun`

### Tier C. Rich note memory
Промежуточный semantic layer:

- `Note`
- `NoteSource`
- `NoteEntity`
- `NoteLink`

Нужен для:
- design direction
- rationale
- local conclusions
- plan fragments
- document digests
- lessons

### Tier D. Raw evidence
Immutable или почти immutable слой:
- chat turns
- tool outputs
- imported records

### Tier E. Retrieval indexes
Даже если физически все сидит в одном Postgres:
- vector indexes на `sources`, `notes`, `claims`, `episodes`
- full-text indexes на `content_text`, `summary`, `normalized_text`
- filters по `namespace`, `entity`, `status`, `retrieval_state`, `time`, `type`

## 6. Structured scope

`v2` уже хорошо чувствовал важность scope, но `v3` делает это явным.

Нужно хранить одновременно:

- `scope_text`
  - человеческое объяснение границ
- `scope_json`
  - машинно-обрабатываемый envelope

Минимум в `scope_json`:
- `scope_kind`
- `project_id`
- `environment`
- `subject_entity_id`
- `conversation_id`
- `modality`
- `audience`

Почему это важно:
- reconciliation становится предсказуемее
- retrieval меньше переобобщает
- notes не конфликтуют ложно только потому, что обе “про базу”

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.

- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.

Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope
Document lines: 127-208

## 5. Storage tiers

### Tier A. Core / in-context memory
Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt.

Что туда класть:
- краткий user profile
- active tasks
- очень короткий session summary
- system-level behavior preferences

Что туда не класть:
- все claims подряд
- все notes подряд
- raw transcript
- длинные списки фактов

### Tier B. Structured long-term memory
Основное typed memory-хранилище.

Типы:
- `Entity`
- `Claim`
- `Profile`
- `Task`
- `Episode`
- `Source`
- `MemoryRun`

### Tier C. Rich note memory
Промежуточный semantic layer:

- `Note`
- `NoteSource`
- `NoteEntity`
- `NoteLink`

Нужен для:
- design direction
- rationale
- local conclusions
- plan fragments
- document digests
- lessons

### Tier D. Raw evidence
Immutable или почти immutable слой:
- chat turns
- tool outputs
- imported records

### Tier E. Retrieval indexes
Даже если физически все сидит в одном Postgres:
- vector indexes на `sources`, `notes`, `claims`, `episodes`
- full-text indexes на `content_text`, `summary`, `normalized_text`
- filters по `namespace`, `entity`, `status`, `retrieval_state`, `time`, `type`

## 6. Structured scope

`v2` уже хорошо чувствовал важность scope, но `v3` делает это явным.

Нужно хранить одновременно:

- `scope_text`
  - человеческое объяснение границ
- `scope_json`
  - машинно-обрабатываемый envelope

Минимум в `scope_json`:
- `scope_kind`
- `project_id`
- `environment`
- `subject_entity_id`
- `conversation_id`
- `modality`
- `audience`

Почему это важно:
- reconciliation становится предсказуемее
- retrieval меньше переобобщает
- notes не конфликтуют ложно только потому, что обе “про базу”

Structured scope', storage tier design"},"summary":"The Memory v3 architecture defines five storage tiers: Tier A Core / in-context memory for small prompt-bound blocks; Tier B Structured long-term memory for typed memory; Tier C Rich note memory for semantic notes and links; Tier D Raw evidence for immutable or nearly immutable source material; and Tier E Retrieval indexes for vector, full-text, and filter indexes even if physically stored in one Postgres.","tags":["doc_digest","architecture","storage_tiers"],"title":"Memory v3 storage tiers separate prompt, typed memory, notes, evidence, and indexes","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null},{"candidate_claim_hints":["Core / in-context memory contains brief user profile, active tasks, very short session summary, and system-level behavior preferences","Core / in-context memory excludes all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists"],"confidence":0.99,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<2>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<1>","role":"secondary"}],"evidence_kind":"summarized","evidence_quote":"Это маленькие блоки, которые почти всегда идут в prompt.","evidence_reason":"The Tier A subsection states the design intent and enumerates what belongs and does not belong in this tier.","importance":5,"keywords":["Tier A","Core / in-context memory","prompt","user profile","active tasks","session summary","behavior preferences"],"note_type":"direction","rationale":"This captures a reusable prompt-context policy and trade-off, not just one clean fact.","scope_json":{"basis":"summarized","kind":"document","text":"agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope', Tier A Core / in-context memory policy"},"summary":"Tier A Core / in-context memory should contain only small blocks that almost always go into the prompt, such as a brief user profile, active tasks, very short session summary, and system-level behavior preferences; it should exclude all claims, all notes, raw transcript, and long fact lists.","tags":["architecture","prompt_context","storage_tiers"],"title":"Core / in-context memory tier is intentionally small and prompt-bound","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null},{"candidate_claim_hints":["Memory v3 stores both scope_text and scope_json","scope_json includes scope_kind, project_id, environment, subject_entity_id, conversation_id, modality, and audience","Structured scope improves reconciliation and retrieval"],"confidence":0.99,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<7>","role":"primary"},{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"secondary"}],"evidence_kind":"summarized","evidence_quote":"Нужно хранить одновременно:","evidence_reason":"The Structured scope section explicitly describes storing both scope representations, lists required scope_json fields, and gives the reasons for doing so.","importance":5,"keywords":["Structured scope","scope_text","scope_json","scope_kind","project_id","environment","subject_entity_id","conversation_id","modality","audience","reconciliation","retrieval"],"note_type":"doc_digest","rationale":"This note preserves the design rationale and required structure for scope handling, which is richer than a single atomic claim.","scope_json":{"basis":"summarized","kind":"document","text":"agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope', structured scope design"},"summary":"Memory v3 makes scope explicit by storing both `scope_text` as a human explanation of boundaries and `scope_json` as a machine-processable envelope containing at least `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience`. This is intended to make reconciliation more predictable, reduce overgeneralized retrieval, and prevent false note conflicts caused by vague shared topics.","tags":["doc_digest","architecture","scope"],"title":"Structured scope combines human boundary text with machine-readable envelope","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null}]}
